CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, data VARCHAR(255), metrics INT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data, metrics) VALUES (1, 'sample1', 100), (2, 'sample2', 200), (3, 'sample3', 300);
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, info VARCHAR(255), count_value INT);
INSERT INTO another_table (id, info, count_value) VALUES (1, 'info1', 50), (2, 'info2', 75), (3, 'info3', 100);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE metrics > (SELECT AVG(count_value) FROM another_table);
SELECT a.id, a.data, b.info FROM test_table a JOIN another_table b ON a.metrics = b.count_value;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE metrics IN (SELECT count_value FROM another_table WHERE id < 3);
CREATE TABLE boundary_test (boundary_id INT, boundary_data VARCHAR(255));
SELECT DISTINCT boundary_data FROM boundary_test WHERE boundary_id = 0;
SELECT AVG(boundary_id) FROM boundary_test GROUP BY boundary_data HAVING COUNT(boundary_id) > 1;
CREATE TABLE temp_table (temp_id INT, temp_value VARCHAR(255));
SELECT a.temp_value, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM temp_table b WHERE b.temp_id = a.temp_id) AS count_value FROM temp_table a;
